The '''CL-3 antistarfighter cluster bomb''' was an anti-[[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] weapon manufactured by [[FreiTek Incorporated/Legends|FreiTek Incorporated]]. A type of [[Cluster bomb/Legends|cluster bomb]], the weapon was employed by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] and later the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]. Disguised to blend in with a [[Starship/Legends|vessel's]] superstructure, the CL-3 released a burst of shrapnel in the initial detonation, followed by a cloud of [[Magnet/Legends|magnetized]] [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]]. 

==Description==
The CL-3 antistarfighter cluster bomb, manufactured by [[FreiTek Incorporated/Legends|FreiTek Incorporated]], was an ovoid blister mounted to the [[Hull/Legends|hull]] of a [[Starship/Legends|starship]], typically the [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]]. Designed to blend in with the vessel's superstructure,<ref name="EGWT" /> disguised as standard weapons emplacements, [[Sensor array/Legends|sensor array]] blisters, and cargo modules, the CL-3 targeted enemy [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] formations that flew past the vessel during combat, exploding and destroying them in a surprise explosive detonation.<ref name="NEGWT">''[[The New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]''</ref>

The CL-3 consisted of an outer blister shell that covered an internal space filled with dozens of [[Magnet/Legends|magnetized]] [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton]] and [[Concussion grenade/Legends|concussion grenades]].<ref name="EGWT" /> When triggered, [[Explosive charge/Legends|explosive charges]] reduced the outer blister shell to shrapnel<ref name="NEGWT" /> which was then propelled out into [[Realspace/Legends|space]] alongside the payload of magnetized [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]]. The debris field of shrapnel and deadly grenades spread over an area of about one hundred [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in diameter; enemy starfighters caught in the field were hit by the shrapnel and the grenades that were attracted to their hulls.<ref name="EGWT" />

The host ship was protected from the explosion of the CL-3 by [[explosive dampener]]s at the base of the cluster bomb. The grenades themselves, while effective against starfighters were not powerful enough to pierce the [[Armor/Legends|armor]] of a [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ship]]. Maintenance crews were capable of replacing cluster bombs in less than ten [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]].<ref name="EGWT" />
